case_title: DEANNA MANZO et al VS. PALACE HOTEL et al

ARBITRATOR CHARLES ALLAN DAVIS APPOINTED. ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION MAR-14-2014 THROUGH JUN-12-2014.
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MANZO, DEANNA MANZO, DOMINIC SERVED AUG-02-2013, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC
ASSIGNED TO ARBITRATION FOR MAR-03-2014. JAN-08-2014 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS MANAGEMENT COMPANY, INC KYO-YA COMPANY LTD. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS
ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT KYO-YA COMPANY LTD., STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: IN THIS SLIP AND FALL ACTION DEFENDANTS HAVE MOVED FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING DISMISSAL OF THE ACTION AS A TERMINATING SANCTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, COMPELLING OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY REQUESTS OF DEFENDANTS AS WELL AS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $3,915.00. THERE WAS A PREVIOUS ACTION INVOLVING VIRTUALLY THE SAME PARTIES IN WHICH THE PLAINTIFF DISMISSED THE ACTION ON JANUARY 22, 2012, AFTER DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS WAS GRANTED BY THE COURT. THE ACTION WAS REFILED ON JUNE 30, 2013 IN WHICH PLAINTIFF'S HUSBAND WAS ADDED AS A PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANTS THEREUPON FILED A RESPONSIVE PLEADING AND DISCOVERY REQUESTS. PLAINTIFFS HAVE FAILED TO DATE TO RESPOND TO THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND MEET AND CONFER REQUESTS. THERE IS NOTHING IN THE COURT FILE ON THE MOTION INDICATING ANY RESPONSE OR CORRESPONDENCE FROM PLAINTIFFS OR THEIR COUNSEL TO THE DISCOVERY REQUESTS OR EMAILS FROM DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL ON THE DISCOVERY OR ON ANY OTHER ASPECT OF THE ACTION THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE BROUGHT AGAINST DEFENDANTS. THERE IS SILENCE AND NO APPARENT ACTIVITY ON THE PART OF THE PLAINTIFFS IN FURTHERING THEIR PROSECUTION OF THE ACTION THEY HAVE BROUGHT AGAINST DEFENDANTS. ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS AND DISMISSING THE ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED WITH PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFFS IN THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF $3,915.00 IS ALSO GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIAN A. COLLEY; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

calendar.matters:
ARBITRATOR JURISDICTION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Off Calendar

ORDER TO ARBITRATION CALENDAR LIST
Off Calendar Mar-14-2014 Set To Order To Arbtitration Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses;
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Colette R. Thomason, Esq., Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley, 650.364.8200, appearing for the Defendants.
No appearance by any party or counsel.

Matter called. Ms. Thomason and the Court conferred regarding the sentence in the Court's tentative ruling beginning with the word "Accordingly". Discussions held. The Court stated that the word immediately following "Accordingly" in its tentative ruling should be "defendants" not "plaintiffs" and ordered the correction. Ms. Thomason presented argument in support of the Court dismissing the action with prejudice. The Court granted Ms. Thomason's request to dismiss the matter with prejudice. The tentative ruling with regard to DEFENDANT KYO-YA COMPANY LTD., STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is adopted as modified: In this slip and fall action defendants have moved for an order compelling dismissal of the action as a terminating sanction, or in the alternative, compelling outstanding discovery requests of defendants as well as for monetary sanctions in the total amount of $3,915.00. There was a previous action involving virtually the same parties in which the plaintiff dismissed the action on January 22, 2012, after defendants' motion to compel discovery and for monetary sanctions was granted by the court. The action was refiled on June 30, 2013 in which plaintiff's husband was added as a plaintiff. Defendants thereupon filed a responsive pleading and discovery requests. Plaintiffs have failed to date to respond to the discovery requests and meet and confer requests. There is nothing in the court file on the motion indicating any response or correspondence from plaintiffs or their counsel to the discovery requests or emails from defendants' counsel on the discovery or on any other aspect of the action that plaintiffs have brought against defendants. There is silence and no apparent activity on the part of the plaintiffs in furthering their prosecution of the action they have brought against defendants. Accordingly, defendants' motion for terminating sanctions against plaintiffs and dismissing the action against defendants is granted with prejudice. Plaintiffs' motion for monetary sanctions against plaintiffs in the total amount of $3,915.00 is also granted. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian A. Colley; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. =302/JPT
